description: Discoverability follow-up to disembark_then_advance_11e: after a tactical disembark from a transport that hasn't moved, the unit is 'then selected to make a normal or advance move' (18.04) but nothing told the player — on the pad the Move/Advance/Stay Still menu only opens on an A / D-pad press. This scenario proves the new clarity prompt fires: after CONFIRM_DISEMBARK the MovementController's post-disembark UI update shows an on-screen toast telling the player the unit can still move, tailored to the active input device (mouse vs controller), and refreshes the pad hint bar so its 'Move Menu' chips show immediately. A clean stationary Trukk + Boyz squad (owner 2) is injected on an empty board so the unit is genuinely eligible to move after disembarking.

steps.comment: Inject a stationary Trukk carrying a Boyz squad (owner 2 = active player) on clear board (1600,700), then refresh the phase snapshot so get_unit() sees them.

Mouse/keyboard surface: clear any prior toasts, drive the real post-disembark UI update (the disembark-completed handler calls this), then assert a prompt toast naming the unit and the Move/Advance/Stay Still choice is shown.
Controller surface: with the pad active, the prompt instead names the button (A / D-pad) that opens the Move menu — the reported controller gap.

Pad hint bar refreshed to the movement set: with the pad active and no panel focus, the A button's chip now reads 'Move Menu' so the disembarked unit's move affordance is visible without a first blind press.
